Is study included in Meta-analysis.evidence: The 'IMPORTANCE' section on page 1 states, 'To our knowledge, this is the first randomized clinical trial evaluating an alternating treatment regimen'. The 'DESIGN, SETTING, AND PARTICIPANTS' section on page 1 also describes it as 'an open-label, randomized (1:1) study (ROPETAR trial)', indicating it is a clinical trial, not a meta-analysis.

Is OS data available.evidence: The 'MAIN OUTCOME AND MEASURES' section on page 1 lists 'survival until first progression or death' as the primary end point and 'time to second progression or death' as a secondary end point. On page 3, under 'Assessments - Efficacy', 'Overall survival (OS)' is explicitly mentioned as a secondary end point. On page 4, under 'Efficacy', it states, 'No significant difference in OS was observed between arms (HR stratified for MSKCC risk categories = 0.90; 95% CI, 0.51-1.58) (eFigure 3 in Supplement 2).'

Is QoL data available.evidence: The 'OBJECTIVE' section on page 1 states the study 'improves quality of life when compared with continuous treatment with pazopanib'. The 'MAIN OUTCOME AND MEASURES' section on page 1 lists 'quality of life' as a secondary end point. The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states, 'No difference in quality of life was observed'. On page 3, under 'Assessments - Quality of Life', it details how 'Quality of life was measured throughout the study course by using both The European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire Core 30 version 3.0 (EORTC QLQ-C30) and Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index-Disease-Related Symptoms (FKSI-DRS) questionnaire'.

The number of arms.evidence: The 'INTERVENTIONS' section on page 1 describes 'First-line treatment consisted of either an 8-week alternating treatment schedule of pazopanib 800 mg/d and everolimus 10 mg/d (rotating arm) or continuous pazopanib 800 mg/d (control arm)'. This clearly indicates two treatment arms: a 'rotating arm' and a 'control arm'. The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 further confirms this by stating, 'A total of 52 patients were randomized to the rotating arm... and 49 patients to the control arm'.

Risk.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states, 'Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center risk category was favorable in 26% of patients, intermediate in 58%, and poor in 15%.' The 'Study Design' section on page 2 mentions, 'Patients were stratified according to the Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (MSKCC) risk categories (favorable vs intermediate vs poor risk...'. Table. Baseline Characteristics on page 4 also includes 'MSKCC risk score' with categories Favorable, Intermediate, and Poor.

Author.evidence: Extracted from the author list on page 1.

Year.evidence: Found in the publication details on page 1: "JAMA Oncol. 2017".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is labeled "Original Investigation" and contains extensive sections like Methods, Results, Discussion, and multiple pages of content, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Stated in the "Study Design" section on page 2: "randomized phase 2 trial."

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The "IMPORTANCE" section on page 1 states it is "the first randomized clinical trial evaluating an alternating treatment regimen".

Number of Arms.evidence: The "INTERVENTIONS" section on page 1 and "Study Design" on page 2 describe two arms: a "rotating arm" and a "control arm".

Treatment Arm.value: Pazopanib 800 mg/d and everolimus 10 mg/d (8-week alternating schedule)
Treatment Arm.evidence: Described in the "INTERVENTIONS" section on page 1 and "Study Design" on page 2 as the "rotating arm" receiving "8-week alternating treatment schedule of pazopanib 800 mg/d and everolimus 10 mg/d".

Treatment Arm 2.evidence: The study design describes two arms: a rotating arm (Treatment Arm) and a control arm. There is no distinct second treatment arm.

Control Arm.evidence: Described in the "INTERVENTIONS" section on page 1 as the "control arm" receiving "continuous pazopanib 800 mg/d".

Type of Agent in Treatment Arm.value: Vascular endothelial growth factor receptor tyrosine kinase inhibitor (VEGFR-TKI) and mammalian target of rapamycine (mTOR) inhibitor
Type of Agent in Treatment Arm.evidence: Pazopanib is a VEGFR-TKI and everolimus is an mTOR inhibitor, as indicated by the "Methods" section on page 2 and general knowledge of these drugs in the context of RCC treatment.

Favorable Risk PFS | Rx (mo).evidence: The document provides overall PFS for the rotating arm (7.4 months) and control arm (9.4 months) but does not break down PFS specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for either arm.

Favorable Risk PFS | Control (mo).evidence: The document provides overall PFS for the rotating arm (7.4 months) and control arm (9.4 months) but does not break down PFS specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for either arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Rx | Overall.evidence: The document does not report Overall Response Rate (ORR) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for the rotating arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Rx | CR.evidence: The document does not report Complete Response (CR) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for the rotating arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Rx | PR.evidence: The document does not report Partial Response (PR) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for the rotating arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Control | Overall.evidence: The document does not report Overall Response Rate (ORR) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for the control arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Control | CR.evidence: The document does not report Complete Response (CR) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for the control arm.

Favorable Risk ORR Control | PR.evidence: The document does not report Partial Response (PR) specifically for the 'Favorable Risk' category for the control arm.

Int-Poor Risk PFS | Rx (mo).evidence: The document provides overall PFS for the rotating arm (7.4 months) and control arm (9.4 months) but does not break down PFS specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for either arm.

Int-Poor Risk PFS | Control (mo).evidence: The document provides overall PFS for the rotating arm (7.4 months) and control arm (9.4 months) but does not break down PFS specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for either arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Rx | Overall.evidence: The document does not report Overall Response Rate (ORR) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for the rotating arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Rx | CR.evidence: The document does not report Complete Response (CR) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for the rotating arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Rx | PR.evidence: The document does not report Partial Response (PR) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for the rotating arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Control | Overall.evidence: The document does not report Overall Response Rate (ORR) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for the control arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Control | CR.evidence: The document does not report Complete Response (CR) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for the control arm.

Int-Poor Risk ORR Control | PR.evidence: The document does not report Partial Response (PR) specifically for the 'Intermediate/Poor Risk' category for the control arm.

N | Treatment Arm.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1 and confirmed in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, which states "Rotating Arm (n = 52)".

N | Control Arm.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1 and confirmed in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, which states "Control Arm (n = 49)".

Median Follow Up Duration (mo).evidence: The document states patients were "followed for at least one year" on page 1, but a specific median follow-up duration in months for the entire study population is not reported. Median PFS1 is reported, but that is a different metric.

Median Follow Up Duration for QoL (mo).evidence: The document describes the schedule for QoL measurements (at baseline and every 8 weeks thereafter, plus end-of-treatment visit) on page 3, but does not provide a specific median follow-up duration for QoL in months.

Sex | Males(%).evidence: Found in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "Sex", "Male", "All Patients", which states "69 (68)".

Sex | Females(%).evidence: Found in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "Sex", "Female", "All Patients", which states "32 (32)".

Risk Category Criteria.value: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (MSKCC) risk categories (favorable vs intermediate vs poor risk, based on Karnofsky performance status <80%, hemoglobin level below the lower limit of normal, corrected serum calcium >10 mg/dL, lactate dehydrogenase >1.5 times upper limit of normal, and time from initial diagnosis <1 year before randomization).
Risk Category Criteria.evidence: Found in the "Study Design" section on page 2, which details the criteria for MSKCC risk categories.

Risk Category Included.evidence: The "Study Design" section on page 2 and "RESULTS" section on page 1 both mention the inclusion of favorable, intermediate, and poor risk categories based on MSKCC criteria.

HrQoL Instrument.value: The European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire Core 30 version 3.0 (EORTC QLQ-C30) and Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index-Disease-Related Symptoms (FKSI-DRS) questionnaire.
HrQoL Instrument.evidence: Found in the "Quality of Life" section on page 3, which explicitly lists the instruments used.

PRO Completion Rate (%) | Baseline.evidence: The document states that Quality of Life was measured at baseline on page 3, but a specific PRO completion rate percentage at baseline is not reported.

PRO Completion Rate (%) | On Rx.evidence: The document states that Quality of Life was measured throughout the study course (on treatment) on page 3, but a specific PRO completion rate percentage on treatment is not reported.

Risk Category (%) | Favorable.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1 and confirmed in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "MSKCC risk score", "Favorable", "All Patients", which states "26 (26)".

Risk Category (%) | Intermediate.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1 and confirmed in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "MSKCC risk score", "Intermediate", "All Patients", which states "59 (58)".

Risk Category (%) | Poor.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1 and confirmed in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "MSKCC risk score", "Poor", "All Patients", which states "15 (15)".

Nephrectomy (%) | Treatment.evidence: Found in the "Results" section on page 3, which states "A prior nephrectomy was performed in ... 17 patients (33%) in the rotating arm". The rotating arm is the treatment arm.

Nephrectomy (%) | Control.evidence: Found in the "Results" section on page 3, which states "A prior nephrectomy was performed in 25 patients (51%) in the control arm".

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Lymph.evidence: Found in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "Metastatic sites", "Lymph nodes", "All Patients", which states "39 (39)".

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Liver.evidence: Found in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "Metastatic sites", "Liver", "All Patients", which states "11 (11)".

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Lung.evidence: Found in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "Metastatic sites", "Lung", "All Patients", which states "69 (68)".

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Bone.evidence: Found in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "Metastatic sites", "Bone", "All Patients", which states "35 (35)".

Organs Involved at Baseline (%) | Brain.evidence: Found in "Table. Baseline Characteristics" on page 4, under "Metastatic sites", "Brain", "All Patients", which states "1 (1)".

Primary Endpoint(s).value: survival until first progression or death
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: Found in the "MAIN OUTCOME AND MEASURES" section on page 1.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: time to second progression or death, toxic effects, and quality of life
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: Found in the "MAIN OUTCOME AND MEASURES" section on page 1.

OS (mo) | Rx.evidence: Found in the "Efficacy" section on page 4, stating "Median OS for ... 35 months (95% CI > 12.2) for the rotating arm".

OS (mo) | Control.evidence: Found in the "Efficacy" section on page 4, stating "Median OS for the control arm was 18.5 months (95% CI > 14.7)".

PFS (mo) | Rx.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1 and "Efficacy" section on page 4, and Figure 2A, all stating "Median PFS1 was 7.4 months (95% CI, 5.6-18.4) for the rotating arm".

PFS (mo) | Control.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1 and "Efficacy" section on page 4, and Figure 2A, all stating "Median PFS1 was ... 9.4 months (95% CI, 6.6-11.9) for the control arm".

ORR (%) Rx | Overall.evidence: The document does not report Overall Response Rate (ORR) for the Rx (rotating) arm.

ORR (%) Rx | CR.evidence: The document does not report Complete Response (CR) for the Rx (rotating) arm.

ORR (%) Rx | PR.evidence: The document does not report Partial Response (PR) for the Rx (rotating) arm.

ORR (%) Control | Overall.evidence: The document does not report Overall Response Rate (ORR) for the Control arm.

ORR (%) Control | CR.evidence: The document does not report Complete Response (CR) for the Control arm.

ORR (%) Control | PR.evidence: The document does not report Partial Response (PR) for the Control arm.

All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Rx.evidence: The document reports on "possibly related serious AEs" but does not provide a percentage for "All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%)" for the Rx arm.

All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Contro.evidence: The document reports on "possibly related serious AEs" but does not provide a percentage for "All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher (%)" for the Control arm.

TRAE Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Rx.evidence: The document does not provide an overall percentage for Treatment-Related Adverse Events (TRAE) Grade 3 or Higher for the Rx arm. It only lists specific TRAEs and their prevalence.

TRAE Grade 3 or Higher (%) | Control.evidence: The document does not provide an overall percentage for Treatment-Related Adverse Events (TRAE) Grade 3 or Higher for the Control arm. It only lists specific TRAEs and their prevalence.

TRAE Grade 5 (%) | Rx.evidence: The document does not explicitly report the percentage of TRAE Grade 5 for the Rx arm.

TRAE Grade 5 (%) | Control.evidence: The document does not explicitly report the percentage of TRAE Grade 5 for the Control arm.

No of Deaths | Rx.evidence: Derived from Figure 1 (CONSORT Flow Diagram) on page 5: 1 patient died before first dose, 2 patients died during first-line treatment, and 2 patients died during second-line treatment in the rotating arm.

No of Deaths | Control.evidence: Derived from Figure 1 (CONSORT Flow Diagram) on page 5: 3 patients died during first-line treatment and 4 patients died after first PD (without initiating second-line) in the control arm.

Significant QoL Benefit for Rx.evidence: Found in the "RESULTS" section on page 1, stating "No difference in quality of life was observed." and in the "Discussion" section on page 6, stating "Unexpectedly, QoL was not improved by rotating both drugs."
